meta.title: Ideal Client Profile — Simon Jacobs / Jacobs Taxes
meta.source: Transcribed from docs/ideal-client-profile.md + refinements locked during Gate 1 (June 2026).
meta.note: Single source of truth for who the site speaks to. Copy, structure, and CTAs map back to the pains, goals, and attributes below.

profile.industry: UK marketing / digital agency (hard niche — confirmed by Simon as the main focus).
profile.companySize: Roughly 1–20 core employees, often plus external freelancers/contractors (e.g. ~18 staff + ~20 externals).

annualTurnover.note: Scorecard + form use £500k–£2.5m; original ICP doc said £500k–£5m. £2.5m treated as canonical.
annualTurnover.profitability: ~30% profit before tax where healthy (≈ £150k–£750k PBT on band).

profile.buyingProcess: Finds accountants/tax advisers via social media or referral, then books a discovery call. Decides fast.
profile.decisionMaker: The founder/CEO — typically late 20s / early 30s, sole decision maker, and the lead salesperson.
painPoints: High taxes — corporation, VAT, and personal/director.
High costs/expenses; cost-sensitive in a saturated market.
Accounting illiteracy — nobody on the team came through tax/finance.
Currently has no accountant, OR over-relies on AI (ChatGPT/Claude) for compliance and advice.
Burned by a previous accountant — wanted better compliance AND advisory.
businessGoals: Keep more of what they earn.
Build toward a profitable exit (5–10 year horizon, strong valuation).
attributes: Cut-throat about cutting costs that run too high.
Tech-savvy and forward-thinking (to a fault — trusts AI with tax).

avatar.framing: Hazem's own agency used as the working avatar ('let's say it's us').
avatar.snapshot: Founder-led UK marketing agency, ~£500k–£1m+ turnover (~$100–250k/quarter), ~18 staff + ~20 externals, CEO is the lead salesperson, none of the team trained in tax/accounting.
avatar.currentState: Either no accountant, or DIY-ing compliance and advisory with AI tools like Claude/ChatGPT — which is risky and they half-know it.
avatar.dreamState: Keep more profit, run profitably, and build a clean, valuable business they can sell well.
avatar.trigger: Actively shopping for an accountant: didn't like the old one, wants better compliance and proactive advisory.
avatar.namedPersona: Kajus, 19, Lithuanian — started young via courses → cold calling → ~$20k/mo with a team. The aspirational IDENTITY/VOICE, not the literal buyer.
avatar.outsideForces: Active War Room member (Tate's private network); bought-in and winning. Aligned POSITIVELY with the hustle/guru ecosystem (Tate, Gadzhi, Hormozi) — copy speaks with this worldview, never against it.
avatar.buyerVsIdentity: Band resolved (option C): the actual buyer is a War-Room-minded founder running a £500k–£2.5m agency; Kajus is the younger aspirational identity. Scorecard gate (£500k–£2.5m) unchanged.

antiPersona.description: Who Simon would rather NOT take.
examples: Non-agency clients that dilute the niche (e.g. the dentist + landlord testimonials are being dropped from the site).
Pre-revenue / very small businesses below the band that only need cheap compliance.
Price-only shoppers who want the cheapest filing, not advice.

positioningImplications: Sharpen the target to founder-led UK digital/marketing agencies, £500k–£2.5m — not generic 'agencies'.
Lead with the exit, not just the year-end: build a more valuable, sellable business.
Speak to the three pains: corporation/VAT/personal tax, high costs in a saturated market, competitiveness.
Match the buyer's tempo — punchy, skimmable copy; one obvious path to a discovery call.
Address the sole founder directly in second person ('you', 'your agency').
Trust signals matter (CTA·ACA·ex-PwC) because they arrive cold from social/referral.
Counter AI-reliance directly ('Stop using Claude for taxes') — the avatar literally does this.
copyHooks: Built to keep more profit and a more valuable agency to sell.
Plan tax around your exit, not just your year-end.
Corporation tax, VAT, and what you actually take home — handled together.
For founder-led UK digital agencies scaling toward an exit.
CTA: Book a discovery call (15-min fit call).

settled.noPriceList: No prices on the site (Simon's call; confirmed by competitor study — none of the rivals publish full prices). Reference numbers: £100/hr calls; books £150–£400/mo; tax returns on top.
settled.hardNiche: Single ICP = UK marketing agencies. Do not dilute into influencers/landlords/etc.

openQuestions: AI-agency sub-focus: hard niche or strongest example?
